---
title: "Behavioral Analyses"
description: "Standard analyses for free recall data"
---

jaxcmr provides functions for computing standard behavioral measures from free recall experiments. Each analysis can be applied to both empirical data and model simulations.

## Analysis Categories

### Serial Position

How recall probability varies with study position.

| Analysis | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [SPC](serial-position/spc.qmd) | Serial Position Curve - recall probability by position |
| [PNR](serial-position/pnr.qmd) | Probability of Nth Recall - what position is recalled first, second, etc. |
| [SRAC](serial-position/srac.qmd) | Subject Relative Accuracy Curve |

### Temporal Contiguity

How recall transitions relate to study order.

| Analysis | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [CRP](temporal-contiguity/crp.qmd) | Conditional Response Probability - transition probabilities by lag |
| [DistCRP](temporal-contiguity/distcrp.qmd) | Distance-based CRP - transitions by semantic distance |

### Category Effects

Effects of item categories on recall.

| Analysis | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [CatCRP](category/catcrp.qmd) | Category CRP - within vs. between category transitions |
| [CatSPC](category/cat-spc.qmd) | Category SPC - serial position by category |

### Repetition Effects

Analyses for experiments with repeated items.

| Analysis | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [RepCRP](repetition/repcrp.qmd) | Repetition CRP - transitions involving repeated items |
| [Compound Cueing](repetition/compound-cueing.qmd) | Effects of compound cues on retrieval |

### Error Analyses

Patterns of recall errors.

| Analysis | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Intrusions](error-analyses/intrusion.qmd) | Extra-list intrusion rates |
| [Omissions](error-analyses/omission.qmd) | Items not recalled |

## Using Analyses

All analysis functions follow a similar pattern:

```python
from jaxcmr.analyses.spc import spc, plot_spc

# Compute analysis
result = spc(data, list_length, trial_mask=None)

# Or use the plotting function
plot_spc(
    datasets=[data, sim],
    trial_masks=[mask, mask],
    labels=["Data", "Model"],
)
```

## Comparing Model and Data

Most plotting functions accept multiple datasets for comparison:

```python
from jaxcmr.analyses.crp import plot_crp

plot_crp(
    datasets=[simulation, empirical_data],
    trial_masks=[sim_mask, data_mask],
    labels=["CMR Model", "Observed"],
)
```

## Available in Templates

Each analysis has a corresponding template notebook in `templates/`:

- `templates/spc.ipynb`
- `templates/crp.ipynb`
- `templates/pnr.ipynb`
- etc.

These templates show complete workflows for computing and visualizing each measure.
